steps.title: Focus on ranked lists
steps.content: The researchers concluded that people reviewing ranked lists tend to focus on the top-ranked option. Supporting data must therefore come from the ranked group and show extra attention to the museum ranked first, the British Museum.

steps.title: Use the relevant group
steps.content: Choice A and Choice D describe participants without rankings, so they do not test the conclusion. Choice C discusses the Natural History Museum, not the top-ranked option. The ranked group spending disproportionately more time on the British Museum supports the claim. The answer is B.
